claims.correction: Matches official government rhetoric used to justify the protective conditions.

claims.claim: BYD's Atto 3 and Dolphin currently sell as CBU imports at RM130K-170K
claims.correction: The Atto 3 fits this range (RM123K-167K), though the Dolphin is lower (RM100K-125K). Overall accurate for the affordable segment.

claims.claim: MITI RM200K floor price for CKD production and 80% export requirement
claims.source: MITI Minister statement via Malay Mail & Lowyat.net (Mar 30, 2026)
claims.correction: Specifically confirmed as the Johari Ghani conditions for BYD Tanjung Malim entry.

claims.claim: BYD is the world's largest EV maker by volume (4.2M+ vehicles in 2025)
claims.correction: Verified. BYD surpassed Tesla in total EV/PHEV volume globally in 2024/2025.

claims.claim: Proton is 49.9% owned by Geely and Perodua is 25.2% owned by Daihatsu
claims.source: DRB-HICOM Annual Reports & Sime Darby/UMW structure

claims.claim: Thailand approved BYD Rayong factory with no export quotas and no price floors
claims.source: Thailand Board of Investment (BOI) EV 3.0 Policy
claims.correction: Thailand requires a 1:1 local production-to-import offset but does not mandate export destinations or domestic price floors for CKD units.

claims.claim: KLK TechPark RM3.5 billion GDV projection
claims.source: KLK Land corporate filings & The Star

claims.claim: CKD EVs enjoy 100% sales and excise tax exemption until 2027

omitted_facts: MITI is reinstating a RM250,000 price floor for CBU EVs starting in 2026 to force brands into local assembly.
Sime Darby RM3.57 billion acquisition of UMW consolidated Perodua under PNB ultimate control, capturing ~60% of TIV.
Proton e.MAS 7 launched with high local content and aggressive pricing (~RM100k-120k) to compete directly with BYD.
source_assessment: Robust mix of mainstream Malaysian news and specialized automotive portals. Correctly synthesizes ministerial statements with long-standing industry ownership data. Regional competition data adds necessary context.
overall_assessment: Exceptionally reliable. Accurately captures the 80/20 and RM200k conditions reported on March 30, 2026. Minor nuance on Dolphin pricing does not invalidate the core economic argument.
